name: Washington (United States)

colors.symbolism: the evergreen forests for which Washington is nicknamed

description: A dark green field with the Seal of the State of Washington — a portrait of George Washington on a blue disc encircled by the inscription 'THE SEAL OF THE STATE OF WASHINGTON 1889' on a yellow ring — in the centre.
history: Washington is the only U.S. state flag with a green field and the only one bearing the image of an individual person. The state flag was officially adopted on 7 March 1923, although the green-with-seal design had been in unofficial use since the late nineteenth century. The seal was redesigned by Richard Nelms in 1967 to standardize the portrait.

features.role: the Seal of the State of Washington
features.symbolism: depicts a portrait of George Washington (after Gilbert Stuart's Athenaeum portrait) on a blue disc, encircled by the words 'THE SEAL OF THE STATE OF WASHINGTON 1889'
